title: Fractions
learning_objectives: Add and subtract fractions using a common denominator; simplify results.
Multiply fractions and divide by multiplying by the reciprocal.
Convert between mixed numbers and improper fractions.
Find a fraction 'of' a quantity, including a fraction of what remains.
Compare fractions quickly using a common denominator or benchmarks.

pedagogical_model.sequence: I do → we do → you do

opening.builds_on: Factors and LCM (for common denominators) and division.

items.prompt: What is the LCM of 4 and 6?

items.answer: The pieces are different sizes; you must use a common denominator first.

retrieval_starter.purpose: Retrieves simplifying, same-denominator addition, and LCM — the prerequisites for adding unlike fractions and for the 'fraction of a quantity' problems the GMAT loves.
retrieval_starter.if_students_struggle: If LCM is shaky, recap with multiples lists (4: 4,8,12 …; 6: 6,12) before today's unlike-denominator work; the whole topic rides on it.

questions.prompt: What is the LCM of 4 and 6?

opening.prior_knowledge_bridge: You added 3/8 + 1/8 easily because the pieces were the same size. The Do-Now's last question is the whole point of today: when denominators differ, you can't just add tops. You'll use the LCM skill you just retrieved to make the pieces the same size first — then multiply, divide, and take fractions of quantities the way GMAT word problems demand.
opening.learning_intention: By the end you can add, subtract, multiply, and divide fractions (including mixed numbers) and compute a fraction of a quantity — simplifying as you go.
success_criteria: I can combine two unlike fractions by rewriting them over a common denominator.
I can divide fractions by multiplying by the reciprocal.
I can answer a 'fraction of a quantity' word problem in one clean setup.

opening_script.move: Do Now on screen; silent retrieval of Q1–5.
Check answers; dwell on Q5 — different-sized pieces need a common denominator.
Bridge: reuse LCM to make pieces equal; preview 'of' = multiply.
I do: the partially-emptied jar problem.

i_do.stem: A jar is 3/4 full of water. If 1/3 of the water is poured out, what fraction of the jar is now filled?
i_do.explanation: Pouring out 1/3 of the water leaves 2/3 of it. Remaining = 2/3 × 3/4 = 6/12 = 1/2 of the jar.
think_aloud_steps: Removing 1/3 of the water leaves 2/3 of the water.
Simplify 6/12 = 1/2. The jar is half full.
i_do.key_takeaway: 'Of' means multiply; 'a fraction of what's left' multiplies the leftover fraction, not the original amount.

scaffold_hints: Find the LCD of 6 and 8 — it's 24.

immediate_feedback.if_incorrect: Don't subtract numerators and denominators separately. Convert both to /24 first.

we_do.explanation: Dividing by a fraction = multiplying by its reciprocal: 2/3 ÷ 4/9 = 2/3 × 9/4 = 18/12 = 3/2.
scaffold_hints: Keep–change–flip: dividing by 4/9 means multiplying by 9/4.

immediate_feedback.if_incorrect: 8/27 is the trap from multiplying instead of inverting. Flip the divisor: × 9/4.

you_do.stem: On a test, 3/5 of the questions are quantitative and 1/4 of the remaining questions are geometry. What fraction of all the questions are geometry?
Which of these is the greatest: 5/8, 7/12, or 2/3?
If 3/4 of a number is 18, what is 5/6 of the same number?
A tank is 2/5 full. After adding 12 liters it is 7/10 full. What is the tank's full capacity, in liters?
you_do.explanation: The added water is 7/10 − 2/5 = 7/10 − 4/10 = 3/10 of the tank = 12 L, so capacity = 12 ÷ 3/10 = 40 liters.

sample_held_out_items.stem: If 2/3 of a number is 10, what is the number?

mastery_check.definition: A topic is mastered at >=85% correct on new/held-out-style questions across >=2 spaced sessions with delay (PRD Section 9.2). Flashcard recall alone is not mastery; application under mixed, delayed conditions is the bar.
conditions: >=85% correct on new/held-out-style items (not flashcard recall) per session
sustained across >=2 spaced sessions with a delay between them
per-topic memory surfaced only after >=20 graded reviews (PRD give-up rule)
per-topic performance surfaced only after >=8 application attempts (PRD give-up rule)
mastery_check.on_fail: Not yet: route every miss to the required error log (tag why: content gap / reasoning flaw / timing / careless / trap), schedule spaced re-practice via topic-aware scheduling, then re-enter the we-do -> you-do loop before re-testing.
